65	// MergeMachineConfigs combines multiple machineconfig objects into one object.
66	// It sorts all the configs in increasing order of their name.
67	// It uses the Ignition config from first object as base and appends all the rest.
68	// Kernel arguments are concatenated.
69	// It defaults to the OSImageURL provided by the CVO but allows a MC provided OSImageURL to take precedence.
70	func MergeMachineConfigs(configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, cconfig *mcfgv1.ControllerConfig, imageStream *mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet) (*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
71		if len(configs) == 0 {
72			return nil, nil
73		}
74	
75		// Overall the sort is alphanumerical, but custom pool configuration should take priority.
76		// Generally speaking if a custom pool is created, the expectation is that custom pool configuration should override base
77		// worker configuration.
78		// This mostly aims to help with generated configs (e.g. kubelet or containerruntime configs) where the pool name is
79		// part of the MachineConfig name, which cannot be directly modified.
80		var workerConfigs, otherConfigs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig
81		for _, config := range configs {
82			if config.ObjectMeta.Labels == nil {
83				// This shouldn't really be possible
84				return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot find label in MachineConfig %s", config.ObjectMeta.Name)
85			}
86			if config.ObjectMeta.Labels[MachineConfigRoleLabel] == MachineConfigPoolWorker {
87				workerConfigs = append(workerConfigs, config)
88			} else {
89				otherConfigs = append(otherConfigs, config)
90			}
91		}
92		sort.SliceStable(workerConfigs, func(i, j int) bool { return workerConfigs[i].Name < workerConfigs[j].Name })
93		sort.SliceStable(otherConfigs, func(i, j int) bool { return otherConfigs[i].Name < otherConfigs[j].Name })
94		configs = append(configs[:0], append(workerConfigs, otherConfigs...)...)
95	
96		var fips bool
97		var kernelType string
98		var outIgn ign3types.Config
99		var err error
100	
101		if configs[0].Spec.Config.Raw == nil {
102			outIgn = ign3types.Config{
103				Ignition: ign3types.Ignition{
104					Version: ign3types.MaxVersion.String(),
105				},
106			}
107		} else {
108			outIgn, err = ParseAndConvertConfig(configs[0].Spec.Config.Raw)
109			if err != nil {
110				return nil, err
111			}
112		}
113	
114		for idx := 1; idx < len(configs); idx++ {
115			if configs[idx].Spec.Config.Raw == nil {
116				continue
117			}
118	
119			mergedIgn, err := ParseAndConvertConfig(configs[idx].Spec.Config.Raw)
120			if err != nil {
121				return nil, err
122			}
123	
124			// Ignition merge does not merge the compression field and the content together,
125			// leading to mismatches between the value of the compression and the content.
126			// This is specially notorious when this loop merges a content with the gzip compression
127			// set for a file and the next iteration overrides the file content but without compression.
128			// The merge output will have the proper content set but, as both fields are mapped separately,
129			// but the compression will be the one from previous merges.
130			// To avoid that behavior this logic makes all files have the compression field, if not set, set to empty.
131			// The empty value will always override the last compression algorithm set in previous merges. After the
132			// merge is done we can safely set the "empty" compression algorithms back to nil.
133			// See https://github.com/coreos/butane/issues/332
134			ignitionMergeSetFilesDefaultCompression(&mergedIgn)
135			outIgn = ign3.Merge(outIgn, mergedIgn)
136			ignitionMergeUnsetFilesDefaultCompression(&outIgn)
137		}
138	
139		// For file entries without a default overwrite, set it to true
140		// The MCO will always overwrite any files, but Ignition will not,
141		// Causing a difference in behaviour and failures when scaling new nodes into the cluster.
142		// This was a default change from ign spec2->spec3 which users don't often specify.
143		for idx := range outIgn.Storage.Files {
144			if outIgn.Storage.Files[idx].Overwrite == nil {
145				outIgn.Storage.Files[idx].Overwrite = boolToPtr(true)
146			}
147		}
148	
149		rawOutIgn, err := json.Marshal(outIgn)
150		if err != nil {
151			return nil, err
152		}
153	
154		// Setting FIPS to true or kernelType to a non-default value in any MachineConfig takes priority in setting that field
155		for _, cfg := range configs {
156			if cfg.Spec.FIPS {
157				fips = true
158			}
159			if cfg.Spec.KernelType == KernelTypeRealtime || cfg.Spec.KernelType == KernelType64kPages {
160				kernelType = cfg.Spec.KernelType
161			}
162		}
163	
164		// If no MC sets kernelType, then set it to 'default' since that's what it is using
165		if kernelType == "" {
166			kernelType = KernelTypeDefault
167		}
168	
169		kargs := []string{}
170		for _, cfg := range configs {
171			kargs = append(kargs, cfg.Spec.KernelArguments...)
172		}
173	
174		extensions := []string{}
175		for _, cfg := range configs {
176			extensions = append(extensions, cfg.Spec.Extensions...)
177		}
178	
179		// Ensure that kernel-devel extension is applied only with default kernel.
180		if kernelType != KernelTypeDefault {
181			if InSlice("kernel-devel", extensions) {
182				return nil, fmt.Errorf("installing kernel-devel extension is not supported with kernelType: %s", kernelType)
183			}
184		}
185

192			if cfg.Annotations[GeneratedByControllerVersionAnnotationKey] != "" {
193				continue
194			}

461	// updateMachineConfiguration handles updates to the cluster-level MachineConfiguration
462	// by triggering a reconciliation if the ManagedBootImagesStatus changed.
463	func (ctrl *Controller) updateMachineConfiguration(oldMC, newMC interface{}) {
464	
465		oldMachineConfiguration := oldMC.(*opv1.MachineConfiguration)
466		newMachineConfiguration := newMC.(*opv1.MachineConfiguration)
467	
468		// Take no action if this isn't the "cluster" level MachineConfiguration object
469		if oldMachineConfiguration.Name != ctrlcommon.MCOOperatorKnobsObjectName {
470			klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfiguration %s updated, but does not match %s, skipping bootimage sync", oldMachineConfiguration.Name, ctrlcommon.MCOOperatorKnobsObjectName)
471			return
472		}
473	
474		// Skip reconciliation if neither ManagedBootImagesStatus nor BootImageSkewEnforcementStatus has changed.
475		// BootImageSkewEnforcementStatus is only checked when the BootImageSkewEnforcement feature gate is enabled.
476		if reflect.DeepEqual(oldMachineConfiguration.Status.ManagedBootImagesStatus, newMachineConfiguration.Status.ManagedBootImagesStatus) &&
477			(!ctrl.fgHandler.Enabled(features.FeatureGateBootImageSkewEnforcement) ||
478				reflect.DeepEqual(oldMachineConfiguration.Status.BootImageSkewEnforcementStatus, newMachineConfiguration.Status.BootImageSkewEnforcementStatus)) {
479			return
480		}
481	
482		klog.Infof("Bootimages management configuration has been updated, reconciling enrolled machine resources")
483	
484		// Update all machinesets since the boot images configuration knob was updated
485		ctrl.enqueueEvent("BootImageUpdateConfigurationUpdated")
486	}

490	func (ctrl *Controller) deleteMachineConfiguration(obj interface{}) {
491

493

## NodeDisruptionPolicy Configuration

### MachineConfiguration Naming Requirement

The `MachineConfiguration` resource is a **cluster-scoped singleton** and must be named **`cluster`** — no other name is valid. This is enforced at multiple levels:

1. **ValidatingAdmissionPolicy** (`manifests/machineconfigcontroller/machineconfiguration-guards-validatingadmissionpolicy.yaml`) — any CREATE/UPDATE with a different name is **denied at the API server** with the message: `"Only a single object of MachineConfiguration is allowed and it must be named cluster."`
2. The constant `MCOOperatorKnobsObjectName = "cluster"` (`pkg/controller/common/constants.go:131`) is used everywhere in the operator and daemon to fetch this object.
3. The operator auto-creates it with that name if missing.

---

### NodeDisruptionPolicy Structure

Set via `spec.nodeDisruptionPolicy` on the `MachineConfiguration/cluster` object. Three top-level fields:

| Field | Key | Max entries |
|---|---|---|
| `files` | `path` | 50 |
| `units` | `name` | 50 |
| `sshkey` | — | 1 |

Each entry has an `actions` list (max 10 items per entry).

**Valid action types** (`NodeDisruptionPolicySpecActionType`):

| Type | Notes |
|---|---|
| `None` | Must be used alone |
| `Reboot` | Must be used alone |
| `Drain` | Combinable |
| `DaemonReload` | Combinable |
| `Reload` | Requires `reload.serviceName` |
| `Restart` | Requires `restart.serviceName` |

**Service name constraints** (for `Reload`, `Restart`, and `units[].name`):
- Must end with a valid systemd suffix (`.service`, `.socket`, `.mount`, `.timer`, `.target`, etc.)
- Name part must match `^[a-zA-Z0-9:._\-]+`
- Max 255 characters

### Key Behaviors

- **User policies override built-in defaults** by matching path/name. Unmatched entries are appended. The merged result lands in `status.nodeDisruptionPolicyStatus.clusterPolicies` — the daemon reads from `status`, not `spec`.
- **Reboot is always forced** regardless of policy if the diff involves OS updates, kernel arguments, FIPS, kernel type, or extensions (`pkg/daemon/update.go:749`).
- **File matching** uses longest-prefix match, so a directory path like `/etc/mydir` covers all files under it (`pkg/controller/common/helpers.go:1217`).
